title: Resume a UTF-8 event log from a physical byte cursor
task: A production run ledger paginates a UTF-8 JSONL file using a byteOffset cursor. The first page works, but resuming after an event containing multibyte text can skip or corrupt later events because src/ledger.js applies the byte offset as a JavaScript string index. Fix the reader so byteOffset remains a physical byte position. Preserve the public return shape and fallback behavior. Do not modify the tests. Run npm test to verify.

provenance.source: SeekForge boundary-checklist #131: run-ledger replay cursor defect

notes: A minimized regression from SeekForge's own background-run ledger. It distinguishes byte positions from UTF-16 string indices with CJK payload data.
